extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the sprint after waiting to <100m in headwind while ACKERMANN went early and died, but was DSQ'd for leftward deviation off ACKERMANN's wheel, giving win to GAVIRIA Fernando
tactical_patterns: ACKERMANN forced early into headwind, demonstrating sprinters knew too-early acceleration was fatal in these conditions before 100m marker
DÉMARE Arnaud's Groupama-FDJ teammates swept intermediate sprint podium points when rivals didn't contest, revealing targeted Ciclamino strategy while others conserved
ROGLIČ Primož dropped to 30-40th position in technical Grosseto sections, exposing Grand Tour GC riders' unfamiliarity with bunch positioning in nervous finales

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: demonstrated superior sprint timing waiting until <100m vs early starters, but DSQ suggests aggressive positional changes create jury risk
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: forced to lead out into headwind from >150m, faded badly suggesting vulnerable when unable to follow wheel in adverse conditions
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: team systematically harvested uncontested intermediate sprint points across multiple bonus opportunities while rivals ignored them
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: pushed back 30-40 positions in technical urban sections despite starting at front, revealing positional vulnerability in nervous finales
